text: Final manual review additions from installed smoke: the stats/detail UI still needs a presentation pass even though body blobs exist. Specific observations: model detail drawer shows too much low-level metadata at once and wraps/overlaps long hash/body-size fields; HTTP/DNS details previously duplicated raw JSON plus table rendering and must show one canonical detail view with payload sections; file details expose null-heavy first-party fields instead of only populated action fields; process tab terminology is unclear (`exec` vs `observed`) and should explain/control from route contract, not extra prose theater; credential table should use log verbs (`captured`, `injected`, `brokered`, `error`) and not expose raw blake/hash fields as primary UI; security summary needs detection-level summary alongside action counts. Keep exact DB/route truth, but make normal UI readable and contract-shaped.

Final AGY installed review evidence to preserve before cleanup: live session `co-work-mqpkixnp` generated `/root/poem.md` successfully through AGY/Gemini. Installed `capsem info` showed ~69K input tokens and 2 tool calls. DB inspection explains token count as five `model_calls` totaling 68,991 input tokens / 400 output tokens, with `tool_calls` containing native `list_dir` and `write_to_file` for `/root/poem.md`, while `mcp_calls=0`. This is good evidence for model-native tool extraction, but it exposes two bugs this item/S06-011 must account for: stats UI must render model/tool/file rows clearly, and the top-bar `2 calls` must not imply MCP when they are model-native tools. Also preserve the credential-ref smear bug: unrelated file writes inherited a Google credential ref and must be fixed with event-local credential attribution.

Final AI CLI health review additions: Claude runtime path has mixed evidence. Positive: `ollama launch claude` works in a live Capsem VM and produced a real response. Negative: `claude doctor`/diagnostics report native Claude v2.1.177 but `~/.local/bin` not on PATH, config install method unknown, remote control disabled/auth incomplete, and setup warning says `claude command at /root/.local/bin/claude missing or broken`. Codex review found v0.139.0 with update banner to v0.141.0, bubblewrap missing from PATH, and unexpected Ollama preconfiguration. Doctor/Ironbank must assert real agent health, exact expected versions/config/install method/PATH, sandbox prerequisites, and deterministic model+file task behavior, not just binary presence.

Manual apt failure details from installed VM: remote `apt install htop` over `https://deb.debian.org` failed with `Certificate verification failed: The certificate is NOT trusted. The certificate issuer is unknown. Could not handshake: Error in the certificate verification. No system certificates available. Try installing ca-certificates.` This proves local `.deb` install tests were insufficient. Gate must exercise remote HTTPS apt through the real Capsem network/TLS path and then run the installed binary.

Manual shipped-profile leakage evidence: default Codex session came up preconfigured with Ollama/local behavior, while Ollama should be an explicit profile/user choice and test overrides must stay in Ironbank/mock fixtures. This item must fail release if committed profile roots, generated profile payloads, or baked guest config contain `127.0.0.1:11434`, `localhost` Ollama endpoints, mock server endpoints, or local-model provider overrides without an explicit local-model profile mode. The gate must inspect generated assets/config, not only source templates.

Latest manual release review symptom: even after switching credential storage away from macOS Keychain for the current dev build, user still saw Keychain prompts (`com.capsem.credential`/`org.capsem.credentials`). This means S07-005 must not only check release signing identity; it must audit all remaining Keychain callers and prove dev/test/file-store mode does not touch Keychain, while production signed install uses a stable designated requirement and avoids repeated prompts after Always Allow.

Unblocked by architecture decision: one user/security tool-call ledger is the contract. MCP/native/builtin/local are origins on tool-call evidence, not separate product panels. Current implementation work starts by finding every separate MCP/tool count/projection and replacing user-facing truth with one unified tool surface; MCP transport-only rows either enrich/projection into tool_call or become explicit orphan detection evidence.

Correction from user: do not only unify UI/query projection first. Force the one-table contract in SQLite first. `tool_calls` is the canonical ledger for native/model, MCP transport, builtin, and local tool calls. `mcp_calls` must not remain a first-class tool-call truth; if kept temporarily for non-tool MCP protocol evidence, it cannot be the user/security tool-call table and MCP tools/call must write/query through `tool_calls`.

Contract refinement from manual review: canonical tool_calls rows must attach to model evidence when present. Model-emitted tool calls carry model_call_id for the model response/request row that emitted the call; tool_responses remain linked to the follow-up model request row through tool_responses.model_call_id and same call_id. MCP/direct observations may have NULL model_call_id only as orphan/direct evidence; they still live in tool_calls with origin metadata, never as a separate user-facing mcp_calls truth.

text: Follow-up hardening commit 0783a3ed adds stricter tests after manual design review: tool_responses now must reference a matching tool_calls.call_id in the same trace and must reference the exact model_calls row that consumed the tool response; Ironbank model ledger helper asserts model-emitted tool calls attach to the producing model exchange and tool responses attach to the final/consuming model exchange.

Fixed event-local credential attribution in commit 45d9c9aa. Root cause: fs_monitor looked up trace-level credential refs from TraceState and wrote them onto unrelated file events, so AGY-created files like poem.md inherited Google OAuth refs. Fix removed the trace credential cache and registration entirely; file events keep trace_id via model tool file hints but only carry credential_ref when the file event itself is brokered (for example .env capture). Gates passed: cargo test -p capsem-core fs_monitor -- --nocapture (19 passed), cargo test -p capsem-core trace_state -- --nocapture (7 passed), uv run python -m pytest tests/ironbank/test_model_client_ledger_contract.py -q (8 passed), cargo clippy -p capsem-core --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, git diff --check. S06-010 remains open for installed/manual AGY stats rendering proof.

Source stats/detail presentation slice committed and pushed as `4cb4c967 fix: clarify stats detail drawer`. Scope: detail drawer now separates body ledger metadata from generic event fields, uses a wider/wrapping field grid, renders payload body metadata next to the body section, and compacts rule/event JSON snapshots before display so null-heavy security-event projections do not dominate the normal UI. Gates passed: `cd frontend && pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/__tests__/stats-view-contract.test.ts` -> 19 passed; `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_stats_detail_contract.py tests/capsem-service/test_stats_routes.py -q --tb=short` -> 3 passed; `cd frontend && pnpm run check` -> 0 errors/0 warnings; `cd frontend && pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/__tests__/stats-view-contract.test.ts src/lib/__tests__/session-language-contract.test.ts src/lib/__tests__/plugin-section-contract.test.ts` -> 29 passed; `just test-frontend` -> 408 frontend tests passed plus Astro build; `git diff --check` -> passed. S06-003 remains open for installed UI visual smoke only.

Follow-up contract hardening commit b3a5be77: Ironbank model_ledger now requires model-emitted tool_calls.model_call_id to equal the exact model_calls row whose response emitted the tool call, while tool_responses.model_call_id remains pinned to the later model_calls row whose request consumed the tool result. Updated dev-session-debug skill to state the current contract: model_calls is one request+response exchange, tool_calls is canonical user/security tool ledger for all origins, mcp_calls is optional MCP transport evidence only. Gate: `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_model_client_ledger_contract.py::test_openai_two_tool_calls_have_exact_item_cardinality tests/ironbank/test_openai_responses_ledger.py -q --tb=short` passed (3 passed).

title: Gate tray dashboard/session actions when service is stopped
description: Manual smoke showed `capsem status` reporting Installed=true but Running=false while tray still offers dashboard/session actions. Tray must reflect service availability: dashboard/session/list/open actions are disabled or redirected to Start service when service is down, and must not send users into dead routes or stale session lists. Keep service-start action available and ensure the disabled state clears after service starts.

gates.spec: Tray/menu state test proves dashboard/session actions are disabled or start-service-gated when `capsem status` reports Running=false.
Route/UI contract test proves service-down state does not show stale sessions or dead dashboard entrypoints, and service-up state re-enables them.
Installed tray with stopped service shows only valid start/offline actions; after starting service, dashboard/session actions work normally.

text: Manual smoke note: `ollama launch claude` still reports Claude Remote Control disabled: no claude.ai OAuth token/subscriber auth/profile scope/org UUID, feature flag evaluation disabled by `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC`, GrowthBook disabled. Treat as expected for Ollama/local-model mode unless model interaction fails: Capsem profile intentionally disables nonessential Claude remote-control/telemetry traffic. Follow-up UX/docs may clarify that Remote Control diagnostics are not required for local Ollama Claude usage.

title: Fix Claude PATH and install-method diagnostics in profile shells
description: Manual smoke shows Claude launched via Ollama can answer, but Claude diagnostics still report native installation/config path issues such as user-local Claude missing/broken, ~/.local/bin not on PATH, or install method unknown. Fix the profile/runtime shell path and Claude install metadata so `claude doctor` is clean for supported local/Ollama usage while preserving disabled nonessential remote-control/telemetry posture.

gates.spec: Profile payload/build-chain test proves Claude user-local shim exists, ~/.local/bin is on PATH for interactive shells, and Claude install metadata/config method is not unknown after runtime root projection.

Ironbank AI CLI health test runs Claude diagnostics in a profile shell and fails on missing/broken ~/.local/bin/claude, missing ~/.local/bin PATH, or unknown install method, while accepting disabled Remote Control in local/Ollama mode.
Installed VM `ollama launch claude` works and `claude doctor` no longer reports PATH/install-method setup issues; Remote Control disabled remains acceptable in local mode.

title: Investigate missing Tools row for model-native writes

title: Unify Alt+N session creation with profile naming

Regression test covers Alt+N or its command handler selecting/focusing the newly created session.
Installed TUI Alt+N creates code-N or selected-profile-N and focus moves to the new session.

title: Fix dashboard session list overflow and stale state
description: Manual dashboard smoke shows the session list does not scroll past about five VMs, lacks a refresh control/auto-refresh, and mixes defunct/incompatible sessions with healthy ones. Add scrolling, refresh, and grouped cleanup UX.

gates.spec: Frontend route/visual test renders more than five sessions and proves the list scrolls without hiding actions.
Route/UI contract test proves refresh updates session list state without reinstall or page reload.
Frontend test proves defunct/incompatible sessions are grouped below healthy sessions and expose a purge-all action only for broken sessions.
Installed app dashboard with many sessions scrolls, refreshes, and groups broken sessions below healthy sessions with purge all.

text: Manual AGY evidence: user ran `write me a poem in agy.md`; AGY displayed two model-native tools: `ListDir(/root)` and `Create(/root/agy.md)`, then reported it wrote the poem. The unified Tools ledger/UI must show both tool calls with origin/type, linked model response, and file evidence; empty MCP/Tools panel is red.

Manual screenshot evidence: AGY session `tmp-4` showed two model-native tools in terminal (`ListDir(/root)`, `Create(/root/agy.md)`), but Stats > MCP/Tools panel showed 0 MCP events / 0 allowed / no rows. This confirms the bug affects AGY too and the UI label must move from MCP-only to unified Tools.

title: Tighten installed hot-route latency budgets
description: Manual TUI showed status latency that is too high for in-memory polling. Add stricter installed hot-route measurements for status/list/profile routes: high-sample p50/p95/max, CPU delta, poll cadence, and UI/TUI footer correlation.

gates.spec: Ironbank route probe runs high-sample installed/source hot-route checks for /status, /vms/list, /profiles/list, /profiles/status, and fails on route or CPU regression.
capsem doctor must report hot-route latency histogram and CPU delta for installed service without reading DB or touching slow lifecycle paths.
Installed TUI/UI polling every few seconds shows stable low route latency and no sustained capsem-service CPU burn during AGY/model traffic.

text: User clarified target budget: hot polling routes should be fast enough for responsive typing/buffer reads. Current 5-10ms is acceptable as an interim symptom reduction but not the desired contract. Target should be sub-2ms where possible, close to 1ms for pure in-memory routes under local hammering, with mostly constant latency and no DB/lifecycle work. Poll cadence around every 5s is fine, but each pull must be cheap.

Correction to prior note: not 5 seconds. The intended contract is responsive UI/TUI polling and buffer reads, with hot in-memory route calls around 1-2ms when hammered locally and cadence fast enough not to be visible to users. Lifecycle routes remain separate; status/list/buffer/readiness must be cheap.

Diagnosed manual AGY Tools-empty report against `/Users/elie/.capsem/run/persistent/tmp-4/session.db`: model-native rows were already present in `tool_calls` (`list_dir`, `write_to_file` for `/root/agy.md`) and `model_items`, but the installed session DB had a minimal `tool_calls` schema without the richer UI columns. `TOOLS_UNIFIED_SQL` failed on missing columns, so the panel rendered empty. Added a narrow minimal-schema read path for missing-column failures only, still sourced from the single `tool_calls` ledger and including `mcp_proxy` origin. Gates: `uv run pytest tests/frontend/test_stats_tool_sql.py -q --tb=short`; focused vitest for MCP/stats; `just test-frontend`; `uv run pytest tests/frontend -q --tb=short`; live sqlite proof against tmp-4 returned both AGY tool rows.

title: Replace route-level SQLite reads with memory projections
description: HTTP/UDS route handlers must not open session.db or SQLite directly. Keep SQLite as logger persistence/recovery only; serve status/latest/history/security/stats from memory projections updated by emit/write events. Audit remaining DbReader/session.db uses and add a guard that catches route-owned SQLite access.

text: Committed `28b02403` for the current hot-route memory slice. Gates passed: `cargo test -p capsem-service vm_list_is_in_memory_only_but_vm_info_reads_session_telemetry -- --nocapture` (1 passed), `cargo test -p capsem-service mcp -- --nocapture` (8 passed), and `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_hot_control_routes_have_latency_and_cpu_budgets -q -s --tb=short` (1 passed). Route-health numbers: service p95 /status 0.2ms, /vms/list 0.1ms, /profiles/status 0.3ms, /profiles/code/mcp/info 0.1ms, /profiles/code/assets/status 1.4ms; gateway p95 /status 0.2ms, /vms/list 0.5ms, /profiles/status 0.5ms, /profiles/code/mcp/info 0.4ms, /profiles/code/assets/status 1.6ms. Remaining S05-013 gates are installed/manual doctor and UI/TUI observed-latency proof.

Initial source inventory after `28b02403`: route-level DB reads remain in explicit ledger/detail routes: `/vms/{id}/info` via enrich_telemetry, `/stats` main.db SessionIndex, `/triage?id=`, `/vms/{id}/inspect`, `/vms/{id}/timeline`, `/vms/{id}/security|detection|enforcement/latest|status`, service `/security|detection|enforcement/latest|status`, plugin detail runtime hydration, and `/vms/{id}/history*`. Control routes `/status`, `/vms/list`, `/profiles/*status`, `/profiles/*mcp/*`, profile rule/plugin list routes are now memory-backed. Stronger next step is a single memory projection owned by logger/emitter/recovery, not ad hoc DB opens in routes.

Committed `794caf39`: security/enforcement/detection status/latest routes now read a service memory projection instead of opening session.db in route handlers. Startup hydrates the projection and a background refresher keeps it current. Regression test `security_routes_use_memory_projection_not_session_db` writes a security event, hydrates projection, renames session.db away, then proves `/vms/{id}/security/latest` and `/vms/{id}/security/status` still return the projected row/stats. Gates: `cargo test -p capsem-service security -- --nocapture` -> 2 passed; route-health -> 1 passed with service `/security/status` p95 0.1ms and gateway p95 0.4ms. Remaining route-level SQLite debt is explicit debug/detail/history/inspect/stats/plugin hydration/run-rollup paths.

Committed `84d84ae6`: removed `enrich_telemetry` from `/vms/{id}/info`; session list and session info routes are now lifecycle/storage-only and do not read session.db telemetry. Regression `vm_list_and_info_are_in_memory_only` proves both routes leave telemetry counters empty even when session.db contains file events. Gate rerun: route-health still passed; gateway `/vms/list` p95 0.5ms and security status routes remain p95 0.4ms.

Committed-next slice in progress: `/stats` no longer opens `main.db` on request. Added a `StatsRouteProjection` loaded at service startup, cached as pre-serialized `Bytes`, and switched `/stats` to return that projection. Regression `handle_stats_returns_global_data` hydrates projection, renames `main.db` away, then proves the route still returns the exact global/session JSON. Removed the 1s security projection refresh loop so the service no longer periodically re-reads session DBs for route projections; security routes use startup/recovery projection until the writer-to-projection feed is implemented. Ironbank route health now includes `/stats` for service and gateway; latest gate passed with service `/stats` p95 1.2ms CPU 0.062s/64 and gateway `/stats` p95 1.3ms CPU 0.064s/64. Remaining SQLite route debt is still explicit forensic/debug/history/inspect/plugin hydration and `/run` rollup paths; do not close S05-014 yet.

Committed and pushed `ed5f61c3 fix: serve stats from route projection`. Gates: `cargo fmt --check` passed; `cargo test -p capsem-service handle_stats_returns_global_data -- --nocapture` passed; `cargo test -p capsem-service security_routes_use_memory_projection_not_session_db -- --nocapture` passed; `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_hot_control_routes_have_latency_and_cpu_budgets -q -s --tb=short` passed after adding `/stats` to service + gateway hot-route coverage. Latest measured `/stats`: service p95 1.2ms, gateway p95 1.3ms. This commit does not close S05-014 because inspect/timeline/triage/history/plugin-hydration/run-rollup still contain route-adjacent SQLite reads.

Committed-next slice ready: plugin detail/runtime routes no longer hydrate by opening per-session `session.db`. `plugin_runtime_status` now reads `SecurityRouteProjection` and filters by profile session ids; credential broker runtime/inventory is reconstructed from `credential_observations` and `credential_injections` in projected security events, with duplicate rule rows deduped by event/ref/source. Regression tests rebuild the projection then rename `session.db` away before hitting plugin runtime routes. Remaining source inventory after this slice: explicit triage/inspect/timeline/history/run-rollup plus startup projection hydration still touch SQLite; hot profile/plugin/security/status routes do not.

After `2e3ea91b`, route-level SQLite inventory is now narrowed to ledger/forensic surfaces: `/vms/{id}/inspect`, `/vms/{id}/timeline`, `/vms/{id}/history`, `/vms/{id}/history/processes`, `/vms/{id}/history/counts`, `capsem_triage` scoped DB queries, and `/run` post-exec rollup. These still violate the strict rule if interpreted literally as "no HTTP handler ever opens SQLite". Fixing them properly needs a broader session event projection (exec/fs/net/model/tool/process/security) fed at startup and then by the writer/emitter rail; do not patch these with per-route caches or polling DB loops.

Committed f82b0f5b `fix: project history routes from memory`. `/vms/{id}/history`, `/vms/{id}/history/processes`, and `/vms/{id}/history/counts` now read a ServiceState memory projection. Startup/recovery hydrates projection once from session DB; `/exec` route pushes live exec entries into memory. Added tests: `history_routes_use_memory_projection_not_session_db` writes exec+audit rows, rebuilds projection, renames `session.db`, and proves history/count/process routes still return exact data; `live_exec_history_projection_does_not_require_session_db` proves a fresh exec projection works with no DB file. Gates: `cargo fmt --check`; `cargo test -p capsem-service history -- --nocapture`; `cargo test -p capsem-service use_memory_projection -- --nocapture`; `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_hot_control_routes_have_latency_and_cpu_budgets -q -s --tb=short` (service p95 examples: /status 0.2ms, /vms/list 0.1ms, /stats 1.1ms, profile assets 1.5ms; gateway p95 /status 0.2ms, /vms/list 0.4ms, /stats 1.3ms). Remaining DB offenders from `rg DbReader::open crates/capsem-service/src/main.rs`: scoped raw inspect/query path, `/vms/{id}/timeline`, debug triage session block, and `/run` rollup. Stats/security/history projection hydration remains intentionally startup/recovery-only.

Next offender analysis after f82b0f5b: `/vms/{id}/timeline` is not safe to port by partial composition because Ironbank expects exec/fs/net/model/MCP rows; it needs a unified protocol timeline projection fed from logger/emitter memory, otherwise route freshness regresses. `/vms/{id}/inspect` is worse: it is an arbitrary SQL escape hatch and many Ironbank/MCP tests still use it. Burning it requires a replacement public ledger inspection contract plus Ironbank rewrites, not a silent behavior change. `/triage` session block similarly uses SQL diagnostics and should be projected from explicit triage summaries. `/run` rollup reads session DB after shutdown to update main summary; that needs a one-shot session summary object from the process/logging rail. Do not mark S05-014 done until all four are resolved or deliberately removed with replacement contracts.

Committed 77b07400 fix: keep hot routes on memory projections. Detection latest now filters detection-bearing rows from the in-memory security projection while enforcement latest keeps the full rule ledger. Gateway proxy routine start/ok breadcrumbs moved from info to debug so hot-route HTTP proxying no longer spends file-log work on successful polls. Gates: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p capsem-service detection_latest_route_filters_non_detection_rule_rows -- --nocapture; cargo test -p capsem-gateway proxy -- --nocapture; uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_hot_control_routes_have_latency_and_cpu_budgets -q -s --tb=short. Route-health evidence after fix: service /stats p95 1.2ms max 1.3ms; gateway /stats p95 1.3ms max 1.3ms; gateway status/list/security/enforcement/detection/profile routes all under budget.

Committed eb471087 (`fix: serve timeline from memory projection`). `/vms/{id}/timeline` now filters the in-memory timeline projection and no longer opens `session.db` per request. Focused gates passed after final edit: `cargo fmt --check`; `cargo test -p capsem-service timeline_route_uses_memory_projection_not_session_db -- --nocapture`; Ironbank `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_hot_control_routes_have_latency_and_cpu_budgets -q -s --tb=short` with representative route health: service `/stats` max 1.3ms, gateway `/stats` max 1.4ms, security/enforcement/detection status routes 0.1-0.5ms.

Post-timeline route audit: remaining request-path SQLite opens are `/triage?id` (session block), `/vms/{id}/inspect` (raw SQL against session.db/main.db), and `/run` post-exec main.db/session.db rollup. `/inspect` is the large contract violation: frontend, MCP, docs, and multiple Ironbank tests still use it as a SQL tunnel. Do not silently preserve it as a hot route; replace with typed route contracts or explicitly keep only non-route local CLI/debug file access. Next clean small slice: serve `/triage?id` session block from memory projections.

Committed 81b164a5 (`fix: serve triage session data from projection`). `/triage?id=...` now returns session diagnostics from `TriageRouteProjection`; `session_db_triage` is recovery/startup-only. Regression `triage_route_uses_memory_projection_not_session_db` writes denied net, MCP error, and failed exec rows, rebuilds projection, renames session.db, and asserts exact triage columns/rows. Gates passed: `cargo fmt --check`; `cargo test -p capsem-service projection_not_session_db -- --nocapture` (security/history/timeline/triage route-memory tests all pass); Ironbank route health `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_hot_control_routes_have_latency_and_cpu_budgets -q -s --tb=short` with service `/stats` max 1.4ms, gateway `/stats` max 1.4ms, security/enforcement/detection status routes 0.1-0.4ms.

Remaining hard blocker after /run removal: `/vms/{id}/inspect` is an arbitrary raw-SQL product/API route, not an incidental read. Burning it requires replacing frontend Inspector/Stats usage, MCP `capsem_inspect`, Ironbank helpers, service/gateway tests, and docs with typed ledger/detail routes. Do not call S05-014 fully closed while `/inspect` exists; it is the last request-path SQLite route by design and violates the stronger "no route should need SQLite" contract.

title: Burn old lifecycle semantics from capsem-mcp

This is the same family as missing unified tool evidence: profile/server tool visibility exists, but execution/ledger path is not actually producing canonical `tool_calls` rows. Needs black-box fix before manual AGY/Claude tool accounting can be trusted.

Committed `f34df015 fix: move projection refresh off exec hot path`. title: Store full HTTP/model bodies on the existing DB writer rail
description: Replace preview-only HTTP/model body storage with full body blob records up to 10MB. Use the existing logger/DbWriter/security-event session DB path only; no side database and no route-time SQLite reads. Events must expose content type, byte counts, truncation, blake3 hash, and blob reference.

Focused gates passed: `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_hot_control_routes_have_latency_and_cpu_budgets -q -s --tb=short` (evaluate routes p95 ~1.1-1.2ms service, ~1.5-1.7ms gateway), `uv run pytest tests/capsem-service/test_no_route_sqlite.py -q --tb=short`, and `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_control_route_contracts_exist_for_ui_tui_blocking_and_vm_surfaces -q --tb=short`. Committed `3f4067a1 fix: tolerate stale session counter ledgers`. This answers the DB-service concern by keeping the existing capsem-logger DbWriter/session DB/projection rail as the only durable path: route handlers still consume memory projections, while startup hydration now treats stale/incomplete per-session ledger schemas as empty live counters instead of failing service startup. Focused gates passed: `cargo build -p capsem-service`; `cargo test -p capsem-service --bin capsem-service -- --test-threads=1` -> 197 passed; `uv run pytest tests/capsem-service/test_dbwriter_snapshot_contract.py tests/capsem-service/test_no_route_sqlite.py tests/ironbank/test_single_security_rail.py -q --tb=short` -> 7 passed; `uv run pytest tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py::test_hot_control_routes_have_latency_and_cpu_budgets -q -s --tb=short` -> 1 passed with service hot routes ~0.1-1.2ms and gateway ~0.2-1.9ms; `cargo fmt --check`; `git diff --check`. No new DB service, no route-time SQLite, no side writer.

Broad Ironbank rerun after DB projection hardening passed: `uv run pytest tests/ironbank -q -s --tb=short -x` -> 80 passed in 310.93s. Hot control routes proved no route-local SQLite and bounded CPU: gateway p95 generally 0.2-1.5ms for status/list/profile/security/enforcement/detection/plugin/MCP routes; service p95 generally 0.1-1.2ms. VM lifecycle routes are intentionally slower because they create/pause/resume/stop real VMs; CPU remained bounded (resume/create ~0.08-0.11s service CPU). Commit under this proof: 3f4067a1 `fix: tolerate stale session counter ledgers`.

Current-head S06 source gate refreshed after `3f4067a1` DB projection hardening. Route/UI service batch passed: `uv run pytest tests/capsem-service tests/ironbank/test_ui_route_contract.py -q --tb=short` -> 166 passed, 1 skipped in 276.96s. Frontend gate passed: `just test-frontend` -> Astro check 0 errors/warnings, svelte-check 0 errors/warnings, Vitest 23 files / 403 tests passed, Astro build 2 pages succeeded. This re-proves source UI/stats/detail contracts; item remains open only for installed UI visual stats/detail smoke.

Current-head S06 broad route/frontend gate refreshed. `uv run pytest tests/capsem-service tests/ironbank/test_ui_route_contract.py -q --tb=short` -> 166 passed, 1 skipped. `just test-frontend` -> 403 frontend tests passed plus Astro/Svelte checks/build. This keeps session dashboard/action source contracts green; remaining proof is installed UI/TUI smoke for create/pause/resume/fork/delete/purge and invalid-action visibility.

Installed doctor/provenance gate refreshed after stale installed profile/assets diagnosis. Root cause: local dev asset sync copied `manifest.json` and assets but did not write `manifest-origin.json`, so installed state could drift with no provenance after local syncs. Fixed `scripts/sync-dev-assets.sh` to atomically write `manifest-origin.json` with schema `capsem.manifest_origin.v1`, origin `local-dev-sync`, and source manifest path. Focused source gates passed: `uv run pytest tests/capsem-build-chain/test_sync_dev_assets.py tests/capsem-build-chain/test_profile_payload_contract.py::test_profiles_package_ai_cli_sandbox_prerequisites tests/capsem-build-chain/test_profile_payload_contract.py::test_profiles_package_scriptable_agent_bootstrap_without_local_provider_leakage tests/test_release_doctor_contract.py::test_profile_roots_do_not_force_local_or_mock_model_providers -q --tb=short` -> 8 passed in 0.48s. Installed state verified by `capsem status`/`capsem assets status`: service running, 2/2 profiles ready, manifest origin `local-dev-sync`, assets ready. Installed VM gate passed: `capsem doctor` -> 327 passed in 31.40s, including AI CLI, bubblewrap, MCP, network, apt/remote apt, zstd, sandbox, storage, and workflow diagnostics.

Committed focused fix `ba3316a9 fix: preserve local asset manifest provenance`. Scope intentionally limited to `scripts/sync-dev-assets.sh` and `tests/capsem-build-chain/test_sync_dev_assets.py`; benchmark JSON churn remains unstaged.

Committed/pushed `4799b842 fix: seed claude native install metadata`. Focused evidence: profile payload test was red before metadata, then `uv run pytest tests/capsem-build-chain/test_profile_payload_contract.py::test_profiles_package_claude_bypass_permissions_bootstrap tests/capsem-build-chain/test_profile_payload_contract.py::test_profile_root_manifests_pin_exactly_the_shipped_root_payload tests/capsem-build-chain/test_profile_payload_contract.py::test_materialized_profile_root_payload_matches_source_profile_root -q --tb=short` passed (3 passed). Rebuilt rootfs with `just build-rootfs arm64 code`, synced dev assets, materialized/reloaded profiles to 2/2 ready. Fresh installed dev VM proof: `/root/.claude.json` has installMethod=native, autoUpdates=false, autoUpdatesProtectedForNative=true; `claude doctor` reports `Config install method: native` with no install-method warning. Sprinty item remains open only because its broader named Ironbank/manual gates still need to be run or superseded exactly.

Manual test update from user: TUI keyboard lag now felt fine enough to treat as functionally closed; keep item open only if Sprinty requires formal route/perf evidence or a named gate. Route CPU/latency hardening continues under S05-007.

Focused route CPU/latency/no-hot-SQLite evidence refreshed after S06-016 asset rebuild: `uv run pytest tests/capsem-service/test_no_route_sqlite.py tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py -q -s --tb=short` -> 4 passed in 17.25s. Static guard proves route handlers do not open/reach SQLite. Hot UDS routes p95 mostly 0.1-1.9ms; hot gateway routes p95 mostly 0.2-1.5ms; live VM status/list p95 about 5-8ms with CPU deltas under budgets. Sprinty refused item closure until full broad gates are attached (`tests/ironbank`, `capsem doctor`, `just test`, manual polling proof), so keep item open for final release gate despite focused fix being green.

Follow-up after `4799b842`: full `capsem doctor` exposed a separate profile shell PATH regression. Added red profile payload contract for `/opt/ai-clis/bin` before `/usr/local/bin:/root/.local/bin`, fixed `.bashrc`/`.profile` for code and co-work, refreshed root manifests, rebuilt arm64 code rootfs through `just build-rootfs arm64 code`, synced dev assets to installed state. Fresh VM `code-19` proves `PATH=/opt/ai-clis/bin:/usr/local/bin:/root/.local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin`, `claude` resolves to `/usr/local/bin/claude`, `codex` and `gemini` resolve to `/opt/ai-clis/bin`, and `gemini --help`, `codex --help`, plus `npm install -g --prefix /opt/ai-clis cowsay` all work. Separate signal: `claude doctor` under `capsem exec` was silent/hung for >60s and should be tracked independently from the PATH/install metadata regression.

title: Bound claude doctor under capsem exec
description: After fixing Claude profile install metadata and shell PATH, `claude doctor` launched through `capsem exec` on fresh VM `code-19` produced no output for over 60 seconds and had to be interrupted, while direct CLI PATH/functionality checks passed. Diagnose whether this is Claude remote diagnostic behavior, PTY/stdout handling, network timeout, or capsem exec buffering. The product must not let doctor-style diagnostics hang silently during release verification.

gates.spec: capsem exec <fresh-vm> 'bash -lc "timeout 20s claude doctor"' exits or emits bounded diagnostic output; no silent >20s hang
capsem-doctor AI CLI diagnostic suite covers Claude doctor timeout behavior without blocking the full suite

text: Committed `7fd63bd6 fix: keep ai cli path in profile shells`. Focused gates: red test first (`test_profiles_package_claude_bypass_permissions_bootstrap` failed for all four shell files), then source/materialized root tests passed (`3 passed`). Rebuilt `arm64 code` rootfs through `just build-rootfs arm64 code`, synced dev assets, reloaded profiles. Fresh VM `code-19`: `claude=/usr/local/bin/claude`, `codex=/opt/ai-clis/bin/codex`, `gemini=/opt/ai-clis/bin/gemini`, `npm=/usr/local/bin/npm`, PATH includes `/opt/ai-clis/bin` first. Functional VM proof: `gemini --help`, `codex --help`, and `npm install -g --prefix /opt/ai-clis cowsay` followed by `/opt/ai-clis/bin/cowsay capsem` succeeded.

Diagnosis complete: `claude doctor` is an interactive upstream terminal UI. `timeout 20s claude doctor` writes a full diagnostics screen with `Path: /usr/local/bin/claude` and `Config install method: native`, then waits on `Enter to close`; stdin newline did not close it through `capsem exec`. `claude doctor --help` shows no noninteractive/JSON flag. Correct release gate is the in-VM AI CLI doctor subset, which passed on fresh `code-19`: `CAPSEM_MOCK_SERVER_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3713 pytest -q /usr/local/lib/capsem-tests/test_ai_cli.py --tb=short` -> 20 passed in 0.72s.

reason: No Capsem code fix required. `claude doctor` is an interactive upstream terminal UI with no noninteractive flag; it emits correct diagnostics then waits on `Enter to close`. Capsem release verification should use the noninteractive AI CLI doctor subset, which passed 20/20 on fresh VM `code-19`.

text: Refreshed route CPU/latency gate after `7fd63bd6` profile PATH/rootfs rebuild: `uv run pytest tests/capsem-service/test_no_route_sqlite.py tests/ironbank/test_route_health.py -q -s --tb=short` -> 4 passed in 17.35s. UDS/service hot routes mostly p95 0.1-1.5ms; gateway hot routes mostly p95 0.2-1.7ms; service/gateway security/enforcement/detection latest/status p95 0.1-0.4ms; live VM status/info/list p95 about 6-7ms; lifecycle operations remain seconds for create/resume/stop as expected and low CPU. Static guard continues proving route handlers do not open SQLite on hot path.

Installed doctor gate refreshed on current dev-synced assets after profile PATH/image rebuild: `capsem doctor` -> 327 passed in 31.51s. It proved AI CLI install/PATH (`claude`, `gemini`, `codex`, `/opt/ai-clis/bin`), bubblewrap, profile configs without local-provider leakage, MCP initialize/tools/file/snapshot/http tools, DNS/HTTP/TLS/SSE/local model/websocket fixtures, direct-IP/non-standard-port behavior, apt/remote apt/npm/uv/pip package function, zstd roundtrip, storage/overlay, sandbox, virtiofs, and workflow diagnostics. Slowest check was websocket echo at 5.07s; overall result PASS.

Installed doctor gate now proves the Claude/profile PATH fix in the real image: `capsem doctor` -> 327 passed in 31.51s. Relevant passed checks include `test_ai_cli_installed[claude]`, `test_ai_cli_in_login_shell[claude]`, `test_user_local_ai_cli_shim_survives_runtime_root_mount[claude]`, `test_ai_cli_help[claude]`, `/opt/ai-clis/bin` in PATH, no npm global path leakage, and Codex bubblewrap prerequisite. `claude doctor` itself remains an upstream interactive UI, but the noninteractive product diagnostics are green in the installed VM.
